findings.title: show-sub command no longer prints total proxy count due to accidental line deletion
findings.why_it_matters: The show-sub command is expected to display a summary total of all proxies after listing per-type counts. Removing these lines regresses the command's output, breaking existing user workflows that rely on the total count.
evidence: Diff hunk @@ -318,60 +329,64 @@ shows removal of two lines: '-        std::cout << "-----------+-------+-------------\n";' and '-        std::cout << "Total      | " << std::setw(5) << totalCount << " | All profiles\n";'
The per-type loop in show-sub still computes totalCount correctly, but the value is never printed to stdout in the modified code.

findings.title: Logger::setLevel() writes shared state without mutex protection
findings.suggested_fix: Add a std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); at the start of Logger::setLevel() to protect concurrent writes to fileLevel_ and consoleLevel_.
findings.why_it_matters: Logger::setLevel() modifies the shared fileLevel_ and consoleLevel_ member variables without holding the mutex, while Logger::setFileLevel() and Logger::setConsoleLevel() (modified in this PR) use mutex locks. Concurrent calls to these methods can cause data races and undefined behavior.
evidence: Logger::setLevel() implementation (unmodified in this PR): 'void Logger::setLevel(LogLevel level) { fileLevel_ = level; consoleLevel_ = level; }' has no mutex lock.
Logger::setFileLevel() (modified in this PR) uses std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); before modifying fileLevel_.

findings.title: Potential integer overflow when casting int64_t config values to int
findings.suggested_fix: Add bounds checking before casting int64_t to int, e.g., if (val > INT_MAX || val < INT_MIN) { use default value; }
findings.why_it_matters: If a config value for xray_workers, xray_start_port, test_timeout_ms, or blacklist_threshold is set to a value larger than INT_MAX (or smaller than INT_MIN), static_cast<int> will result in undefined behavior.
evidence: ConfigReader.cpp casts int64_t values from JSON to int using static_cast<int> without checking if the value fits in the int range, e.g., 'config.xray_workers = static_cast<int>(xray["workers"].as_int64());'

residual_risks: Logger::getLevel() and Logger::getFileLevel() read fileLevel_ and consoleLevel_ without mutex locks, which may return inconsistent values if called concurrently with write methods.

ConfigReader::load() does not validate that priority_mode is one of the allowed values (e.g., 'proxy_first', 'direct_first'), leading to potential undefined behavior in downstream code that relies on this value.
testing_gaps: No test case for the show-sub command to verify that the total proxy count is printed in the output.
No test case for Logger::stringToLevel() with edge case inputs like 'LOG_', 'log_', 'log_log_error', or very large integer config values.
No concurrency test for Logger methods to verify that mutex protection prevents data races.
No test case for ConfigReader::load() with invalid type fields (e.g., xray.workers as string instead of int) to verify that type checks work correctly.
